name: mlx_model_loading_v1
description: MLX model loading on Apple Silicon: safetensors parsing, quantization tradeoffs, unified memory management, hardware capabilities, and loading optimizations

entries.notes: MLX model loading lifecycle: safetensors format, quantization levels, unified memory accounting, Apple Silicon hardware, loading optimizations

model_loading_process.description: Step-by-step from file on disk to ready for inference: safetensors header parsing, weight mapping to layer names, GPU buffer allocation via Metal, and dtype conversion

quantization_tradeoffs.description: fp16 vs 4-bit vs 8-bit quantization: quality impact on perplexity, memory savings ratios, throughput differences, group-size effects, and when to use each level

memory_management.description: Unified memory implications on Apple Silicon: VRAM budgeting for models + KV cache + adapters, swap behavior, memory pressure callbacks, and pool management

hardware_capabilities.description: M1 through M4 Max/Ultra: memory bandwidth, GPU core counts, Neural Engine throughput, and how hardware configuration affects model loading and inference performance

loading_optimization.description: Memory-mapped I/O with mmap, lazy evaluation of weight tensors, buffer pooling, LRU model caching, adapter weight sharing, and pre-warming strategies

provenance.review_notes: Technical training data for MLX model loading on Apple Silicon. Covers safetensors format internals, INT4/INT8 quantization with per-group scaling, unified memory budgets, M-series hardware specs, and loading optimizations including mmap, lazy eval, and LRU caching.

evaluation_gates: Safetensors header: first 8 bytes = u64 LE header size, then JSON header, then binary tensor data
INT4 packing: 2 values per byte, low nibble first, signed range [-8, 7]
INT8 quantization: per-group symmetric scaling, scale = max_abs / 127
Unified memory: CPU and GPU share the same physical memory pool — no PCIe transfer overhead
Memory-mapped loading: mmap maps file pages directly; OS handles demand paging
MLX lazy evaluation: computations execute only when results are materialized
Buffer pool: power-of-2 bucketing with idle timeout cleanup and pressure callbacks
